title: poa subumbellata
intro: Poa subumbellata, also known as the little tussock grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae) and is native to New Zealand. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Poa subumbellata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
